content: This streamlined workflow for Level 1 tasks (quick bug fixes) optimizes for speed and token efficiency while maintaining quality.
The Level 1 process flow consists of four steps: Analyze (Understand issue), Implement (Fix the issue), Verify (Test the fix), and Document (Record solution).
Level 1 tasks use a single-file approach to minimize context switching.
Level 1 tasks use a simplified Memory Bank update with minimal overhead.
For maximum efficiency, Level 1 tasks can use an ultra-compact template.
Use helpers to automatically generate documentation.
Use quick templates for common issues such as performance fixes, bug fixes, or quick enhancements.
Level 1 tasks use a minimal verification process, ensuring the fix is implemented, tested, introduces no regressions, and documentation is updated.
Optimize Memory Bank updates for Level 1 tasks by using a single operation.

This optimized Level 1 workflow provides reduced documentation overhead, consolidated Memory Bank updates, a focused verification process, a template-based approach for common scenarios, and a streamlined workflow with fewer steps.
